stories.headline: Hiker discovers human remains in Douglas County, prompting search
stories.summary:
A hiker discovered human remains in Douglas County on Wednesday, prompting investigators and the Douglas County Search and Rescue Team to conduct a broader search of the area, according to the Douglas County Sheriff's Office.

Authorities did not release details about the location of the discovery, the identity of the remains, or whether foul play is suspected. The investigation remains ongoing.

The discovery adds to a busy week for emergency responders across the Denver metro area, which has been dealing with elevated fire danger, high winds, and multiple wildfire incidents.

stories.headline: Auto shop operator seeks community support after Thornton fire destroys roughly 100 vehicles
stories.summary:
Jason Rol, operator of Rol Race Motorsports on West 84th Avenue in Thornton, has launched a GoFundMe fundraiser after Wednesday's grass fire burned approximately 100 vehicles belonging to him and his customers. Rol said many of the cars destroyed were left in his care by customers of his auto restoration shop.

"Seeing the aftermath has been heartbreaking," Rol wrote on the fundraiser page. "Not just for me, but for my customers, my neighbors and everyone who was affected." The fundraiser had raised more than $3,400 — over half of its $6,000 goal — within 21 hours of being posted.

The fire ignited in a grassy area east of Pinnacle High School Wednesday morning during red-flag conditions and spread rapidly, sending a plume of black smoke visible for miles across the Denver metro. Four firefighters and one civilian sustained non-critical injuries before crews contained the blaze at roughly 10 acres about three hours after it started.

stories.headline: Denver area events for Friday, Feb. 27
stories.summary:
Denver and surrounding communities offer a range of cultural and entertainment options Friday evening, including Opera Colorado's Grand Gala at the Denver Performing Arts Complex, featuring cocktails, dinner, a live auction, and performances beginning at 5 p.m. Tickets are $400.

Comedian Michelle Buteau performs at the Paramount Theatre at 7:30 p.m., with tickets starting at $57. The Mission Ballroom hosts electronic artist Drama with Amtrac at 8 p.m., and the Gothic Theatre in Englewood features Denm at 8 p.m.

Additional options include the Cleo Parker Robinson Dance production "We Have a Dream" in Northglenn, the Lone Tree Symphony Orchestra's "Winds of Destiny" at Lone Tree Arts Center, and the Denver Center for the Performing Arts' ongoing run of "The Music Man" at the Buell Theatre.

stories.headline: Nuggets linked to Khris Middleton as buyout market deadline approaches Sunday
stories.summary:
The Denver Nuggets have shown interest in NBA champion Khris Middleton as a potential buyout-market addition, according to a report from NBA insider Marc Stein. Middleton, currently with the Dallas Mavericks, told Stein he is "still weighing his options" ahead of Sunday's deadline to be playoff-eligible with a new team.

Denver Post reporter Bennett Durando separately reported that the Nuggets have been pursuing forward depth on the buyout market, with Middleton among the candidates. The team has identified wing size and ball-handling as priorities in their search.

Middleton, a three-time All-Star who won an NBA Championship with the Milwaukee Bucks in 2021, holds career averages of 16.3 points, 4.7 rebounds, and 3.9 assists per game while shooting 39% from three-point range. Kyle Anderson, another name connected to Denver's search, signed with Minnesota before the deadline.

stories.headline: Avalanche linked to Canucks defenseman Tyler Myers ahead of NHL trade deadline
stories.summary:
The Colorado Avalanche have been linked to Vancouver Canucks defenseman Tyler Myers ahead of the NHL trade deadline, according to Rick Dhaliwal of CHEKTV in Vancouver. The report comes days after the Avalanche traded defenseman Sam Girard and a draft pick to Pittsburgh for Brett Kulak, leaving the team with approximately $9 million in cap space.

Myers, who serves as an alternate captain in Vancouver, offers physical attributes — standing 6-foot-8 and weighing 229 pounds — along with 65 hits and 91 blocked shots on the season. He is also considered a reliable penalty killer, an area where the Avalanche already rank among the league's top units.

The Avs have also been connected to veteran forwards Ryan O'Reilly and Nazem Kadri. Adding Myers would bolster blue-line depth heading into the playoffs, giving Colorado the option to rest key defenders without sacrificing defensive structure.

stories.headline: Mountain highway closures disrupt I-70 travel as snow hits Colorado high country
stories.summary:
Snow and ice forced the closure of several Colorado mountain highways Thursday morning, including a stretch of westbound Interstate 70 between Empire Junction and Georgetown, which reopened shortly before 9:30 a.m., according to the Colorado Department of Transportation.

Additional road closures were in effect across the high country Thursday, with cameras along I-70 showing wet, snowy conditions and backed-up traffic. Heavy traffic was reported in both directions between Silverthorne and Golden.

The mountain snowfall stands in contrast to dry and unusually warm conditions on the Front Range, where red-flag warnings for fire danger have been in effect for much of the week. Denver metro residents heading to the mountains were urged to check road conditions before traveling.

stories.headline: Sierra Space names Dan Jablonsky as CEO after yearlong search
stories.summary:
Sierra Space, the Louisville-based commercial space company, has hired Dan Jablonsky as its new chief executive officer following a search that lasted more than a year, according to the Denver Business Journal. Jablonsky previously served as CEO of Maxar Technologies and has experience leading Ursa Major, both of which are Colorado-rooted aerospace and space technology firms.

Sierra Space is developing the Dream Chaser spaceplane and the LIFE habitat module aimed at enabling commercial space stations. The company has been working toward its first orbital missions and has faced development timelines that have extended over several years.

The appointment of a seasoned aerospace executive is expected to bring added leadership stability as Sierra Space advances toward key milestones in its commercial space program.

stories.headline: Suzanne Morphew's daughters seek return of her remains as murder case continues
stories.summary:
Six years after Suzanne Morphew disappeared in Chaffee County, her adult daughters Mallory and Macy Morphew are fighting to have their mother's remains returned from investigators who currently hold them, according to the Colorado Springs Gazette. Barry Morphew, Suzanne's husband, has been charged with first-degree murder in connection with her death.

The daughters' effort to reclaim the remains reflects the prolonged legal proceedings that have followed the case, which drew statewide attention when Suzanne vanished in May 2020 near her Chaffee County home. Barry Morphew was arrested in 2021, and the case has moved slowly through the courts.

The Morphew case remains one of the most high-profile homicide investigations in recent Colorado history, with the question of the remains adding a new dimension to an already complex legal situation.

stories.headline: Broncos confirm Davis Webb as offensive play-caller; George Paton in final year of contract
stories.summary:
Denver Broncos head coach Sean Payton confirmed at the NFL Combine that offensive coordinator Davis Webb will take over play-calling duties for the 2026 season, ending speculation about the team's offensive leadership structure. The move represents a significant shift in how the Broncos will operate their offense going forward.

General manager George Paton also spoke at the combine and is entering the final season of his current contract with the team, raising questions about his long-term future with the organization. Paton and Payton both addressed reporters as the Broncos evaluate potential additions ahead of the draft.

Denver finished last season on a strong note, winning 11 consecutive games, and will look to build on that momentum heading into the new year with Webb directing the offense.

stories.headline: Rockies earn first spring training win as Brenton Doyle leads victory over Diamondbacks
stories.summary:
The Colorado Rockies picked up their first win of spring training, with outfielder Brenton Doyle leading the team past the Arizona Diamondbacks. The result marks a modest early milestone as Colorado begins preparations for the 2026 regular season.

Doyle, one of the Rockies' most promising young position players, has been a focal point of the team's rebuilding efforts. His performance in spring training will be closely watched as Denver's club looks to show improvement after several difficult seasons.

Spring training results carry limited predictive weight, but early wins can help establish positive momentum and team chemistry heading into the regular season.

weather.forecast: Today in Denver it's gonna be overcast, which is weird because Denver is supposed to be the Sunshine City or whatever they call it. I don't know who decided that but they were probably looking at a different day. High of 67 degrees, which is nice, I'm not gonna argue with 67. Low of 38 tonight, so it's gonna get cold, and the clouds are just gonna hang around up there like they live here now. Wind's gonna hit about 18 miles per hour, which at a mile above sea level feels like the mountains are just blowing directly at your face for fun.

weekendEvents.title: Arvada Winterfest
weekendEvents.description: A free, family-friendly festival celebrating the diverse cultures of the Arvada community, featuring live cultural performances on two stages, ice sculpting, craft vendors, food trucks, and kids' games.
weekendEvents.location: Olde Town Square, 5726 Olde Wadsworth Blvd., Arvada

weekendEvents.title: DGMG Jewelry, Gem & Mineral Show
weekendEvents.description: The Denver Gem & Mineral Guild's annual show features minerals from around the world, ancient fossils, fluorescent rocks, and handcrafted jewelry, with live lapidary demonstrations and $1 grab bags.
weekendEvents.time: Fri & Sat 10 a.m. – 6 p.m., Sun 10 a.m. – 5 p.m.
weekendEvents.location: Jefferson County Fairgrounds, 15200 West 6th Avenue, Golden

weekendEvents.title: Cupid's Undie Run Denver
weekendEvents.description: Participants run a 'mile-ish' in their underwear to raise money for neurofibromatosis research through the Children's Tumor Foundation, with music, dancing, and a post-run dance party. Must be 21+.
weekendEvents.location: Stoney's Bar and Grill, 1111 Lincoln Street, Denver

weekendEvents.title: Four Mile Historic Park Free Admission Day
weekendEvents.description: Four Mile Historic Park offers free admission as part of the Scientific and Cultural Facilities District's occasional free-day program.

weekendEvents.title: Ruby Hill Rail Yard
weekendEvents.description: Denver's urban terrain park for skiing and snowboarding, operated in partnership with Winter Park Resort, features newly designed rails, boxes, and configurations for skill levels from beginner to advanced.
weekendEvents.location: Ruby Hill Park, South Platte River Drive and West Jewell Avenue, Denver
